examples: In short, her entire musical approach was Romantic: intensely personal, full of light and shade, never pedantic.
He has red, freckled skin which accompanied red hair; he was a man of forty, thin, with a pinched face, precise and rather pedantic; and he spoke with a Scots accent in a very low, quiet voice.
His lecture was so pedantic and uninteresting.
As a mere observer, I see my female friends on Facebook engaging in conversations that border on pedantic.
In 1909 Magie tried to get Parker Brothers to market her invention, but the company passed, finding it too complicated and pedantic.
